title: finetune_region.py fixed. Added waste_detection eval
body:
Summary of intent:
- Fix a bug introduced when log2-based size bin labeling was added to finetune_region.py.
- Add (or prepare for) an evaluation workflow for the Waste Detection fine-tuning dataset to monitor improvements (F1 score reported in description).

What I found:
- The finetune_region.py file implements a WasteDetection dataset finetuning loop and maps size labels using a log2-based mapping. The current implementation builds cs_emb (coordinate+size embeddings) and corresponding labels, including a log2-based sizing bin mapping for s_log2_bins.
- There is a potential risk of label/index alignment issues when multiple boxes are processed (the c_idx/s_idx indexing and the way cs_labels are assembled may become misaligned with the logits/labels used for cross-entropy loss, especially after the log2-based mapping was added).
- The PR description mentions a bug fix related to log2 and an added waste_detection eval script. The code currently contains the WasteDetection dataset loader and the finetuning loop, but no dedicated evaluation script is present in this repo snapshot.

Proposed changes (high level):
- Apply a robust fix to ensure the region_loss inputs/labels stay properly aligned when log2-based size bins are included, especially across multiple boxes per image. This includes validating the construction of c_labels and s_labels against c_logits and s_logits.
- Introduce or wire up an evaluation script/module for Waste Detection to compute F1 (as noted in the PR description). This will help verify the stated improvement after finetuning.
- Add small guards/tests around label construction and tensor shapes to prevent silent misalignments during training.

Impact:
- Minimal runtime impact during training once fixed; improved correctness and reproducibility of the waste detection finetuning branch; provides a path to reproduce the reported F1 improvement.

step_plan.summary: Fix log2-based region labeling alignment in finetune_region.py, harden dataset item construction for Waste Detection, and scaffold a lightweight Waste Detection evaluation workflow to compute F1 after finetuning.

steps.operation: Harden alignment between region predictions and labels in region_loss by deriving the number of predictions from logits and validating label lengths; remove reliance on modulo-based indexing that could misalign multi-box supervision when log2 bins are used
steps.reason: To ensure the number of region predictions matches the corresponding labels (c_logits/s_logits with c_labels/s_labels) when log2-based size bins are present and multiple boxes per image are processed, preventing silent misalignments that could degrade training correctness.
steps.side_effects: Adds runtime shape checks and potential assertion errors for mismatches; may require corresponding test updates and minor performance overhead due to additional validations; could necessitate minor dataset wrapping adjustments to ensure consistent batch shapes.

steps.operation: Harden WasteDetection.__getitem__ to consistently return correctly shaped and typed tensors for images, boxes, and labels; ensure compatibility with multi-box inputs and log2 bin mappings
steps.reason: To prevent shape/typing issues during batch construction when log2-based labeling is involved and multiple boxes per image are present, ensuring downstream training receives well-formatted tensors.
steps.side_effects: May require updates to unit tests and data loading code to align with the new return shapes; could slightly affect data loading performance due to stricter validation.

steps.operation: Add a new evaluation module for Waste Detection and wire it into the finetuning workflow by implementing evaluate_waste_detection in a new file and integrating its invocation in main; provide a lightweight F1 evaluator
steps.reason: To establish a reproducible evaluation path that reports F1 on the Waste Detection dataset after finetuning, aligning with the PR description of an added waste_detection eval script and enabling quick feedback after training.
steps.side_effects: Introduces a new evaluation dependency and potential runtime overhead during evaluation runs; requires dataset availability and may require minor CLI or orchestration adjustments; adds tests or test hooks to validate evaluator behavior.
